title: glycine wightii subsp. pseudojavanica
intro: Glycine wightii subsp. pseudojavanica, also known as the Pigeonwings or Pigeon Wings, is a flowering plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Consuming Glycine wightii subsp. pseudojavanica has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves, flowers, and seeds of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
